name: Modern Java In Action
summary: Study graph for modern Java features that shape backend readability, collection processing, null-safety, concurrency, and API-facing code style.

name: Functional Foundations
summary: Topic group covering behavior parameterization, lambdas, method references, and functional interfaces as foundations for modern Java APIs.

name: Stream Processing
summary: Topic group for stream pipelines, filtering, mapping, reduction, collection, grouping, and parallel processing tradeoffs.

name: Null Time Interface Evolution
summary: Topic group covering Optional, date/time APIs, and default methods for safer boundaries and API evolution.

name: Async and Reactive Foundations
summary: Topic group for CompletableFuture, asynchronous composition, and reactive programming concepts relevant to backend integration flows.

name: Behavior Parameterization
summary: Pass behavior as a value so filtering, mapping, or decision logic can vary without duplicating traversal or orchestration code.

name: Lambda Expression
summary: Concise expression of behavior that fits a functional interface target type and can make backend mapping and policy code more local.

name: Functional Interface
summary: Single-abstract-method interface used as the target type for lambdas and method references, including common predicate/function forms.

name: Stream API
summary: Java collection-processing API for declarative pipelines over element sequences, including intermediate and terminal operations.

name: Collector and Collectors
summary: Collection and aggregation mechanism for reducing stream elements into lists, maps, grouped results, or custom accumulated structures.

name: Stream Side Effect Risk
summary: Stream pipelines become hard to reason about when lambdas mutate shared state or hide business effects inside intermediate operations.

name: Parallel Stream Risk
summary: Parallel streams require measurement and stateless operations because shared mutable data, ordering, and blocking work can erase performance benefits.

name: Optional
summary: Container type for modeling possible absence at API or lookup boundaries, reducing accidental null handling when used with a clear convention.

name: Date Time API
summary: Modern date/time types such as LocalDate, LocalTime, Instant, Duration, and Period help model temporal values explicitly in backend code.

name: Default Method
summary: Interface method with implementation that supports API evolution while preserving binary compatibility for existing implementers.

name: CompletableFuture
summary: Java asynchronous computation abstraction for composing non-blocking calls, handling completion, and coordinating concurrent backend work.

name: Modern Java Backend Style
summary: Apply lambdas, streams, Optional, date/time types, and CompletableFuture to clarify Spring/backend code without replacing explicit architecture boundaries.
